headline: Cyberbullying Cases Involving Minors Rise 55% to 4,800
context: MCMC recorded 4,800 cyberbullying complaints involving victims under 18 in 2025 with 3 linked to youth suicides.

cards.big: 4,800 cyberbullying cases involving minors up 55% with 3 linked suicides
cards.sub: School-age victims increasingly targeted through social media and messaging

cards.big: TikTok was platform in 38% of youth cyberbullying cases in 2025
cards.sub: Short video enables rapid spread of humiliating content before moderation

cards.big: Only 280 of 4,800 cases resulted in formal action under existing laws
cards.sub: Legal framework lacks specific cyberbullying provisions relying on general statutes

cards.big: Education Ministry programme reached only 2,200 of 10,000 schools
cards.sub: Programme relies on volunteer teachers with no dedicated budget

cards.big: Cyberbullying scale tests whether child protection keeps pace with children online activity
4,800 cyberbullying cases involving minors — 55% increase demands school-level prevention, not just criminal penalties.
